title: 怪钟疑案
summary:
Detective Hakast and a policeman visit the home of Mrs. McNaughton to investigate the murder of Miss Pemshaw. They are led into the living room by a young woman named Greta. Mrs. McNaughton expresses concern, noting that she heard a scream the previous day but assumed it was caused by the Lymes children, who frequently make loud noises imitating spaceships or atomic bombs.

Upon being shown a photograph of the deceased, Mrs. McNaughton believes she recognizes the man. She recalls a man who previously visited her home to sell encyclopedias or vacuum cleaners, noting that he had annoyed her husband, Angus, while Angus was planting bulbs. She suggests the man might have been driving a bakery truck, though she cannot confirm if she saw him on the day of the murder.

The officers then go into the garden to speak with Angus McNaughton. Angus explains that when he heard the scream, he was working in the garden and assumed it was the Lymes children. He notes that the children often jump fences and chase cats, and mentions that their father, Mr. Rymes, is an engineer who is frequently abroad. Mrs. McNaughton estimates the scream occurred around 2:30 PM. Angus, who was busy making compost and fixing a water pipe, leads Hakast to his compost pile near a shed by the fence separating his property from house number 19. Hakast asks if Angus saw anyone in the garden of number 19 or peering out its windows, but Angus denies seeing anyone.
